mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent, it signals a deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers corporate earnings revisions downward, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of consumer credit (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS decline AND HOUST decline) fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences, or the joint signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight monetary policy, an increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects resilient supply capacity that mitigates inflationary pressures, allowing equity valuations to expand and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
regime_hint: high interest rate environment with sticky inflation
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.2 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-run trend rate of 2.0 percent signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% does not yield statistically significant positive 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) over a 10-year backtest, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after accounting for momentum factors.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI index exceeding 0.4 percent triggers fears of prolonged central bank tightening, which raises the equity risk premium and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL MoM increases above 0.4% are followed by positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal's predictive rank correlation remains below 0.02 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a labor market paradox where job growth is outpaced by labor force participation, signaling weakening wage pressure and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS increase and UNRATE increase fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in at least 55% of historical instances, or the interaction effect disappears when controlling for real GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, a sharp rebound in housing starts (HOUST) acts as a leading indicator for broader economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon as construction and related sectors boost earnings expectations.
failure_condition: HOUST rebounds do not produce positive 5-day forward returns in at least 60% of post-recession episodes, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below chance level when tested against a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 15-year window.
